name: Lincoln Restler
affiliation: New York City Council member (District 33, Brooklyn); chair of the contracts committee
proposals: Keep the council's authority over the building code rather than shifting it to the Department of Buildings.
Retain outside council review of DCAS property dispositions and leases unless other independent oversight is embedded.
Strengthen, not weaken, the council's role in the budget and rainy-day fund process.
Restore year-round outdoor dining through pending council legislation rather than charter changes that could jeopardize it.
Have the Mamdani administration implement Local Law 169 to set crisp procurement timelines instead of automating report sunsets.
quote: Our charter must be strong enough to withstand the next corrupt administration.

name: Frank Morano
affiliation: New York City Council member (District 51, Staten Island)
proposals: Extend this into a two-year charter revision commission rather than an accelerated timeline.
Put his community board reform question on the ballot, letting council members appoint up to half of community board members.
Avoid using charter revision as a backdoor to weaken review of major transportation projects, busways or bike lanes.
Replace removal of council review of DCAS leases with a tiered review system and statutory deadlines that preserve accountability.
quote: I'm asking you to trust the voters enough to let them decide.

name: Edgar Alfonso
affiliation: Resident of Forest Hills/Rego Park and parent; chairs the transportation committee of Queens Community Board 6 but testified in a personal capacity
proposals: Cut the red tape and major-transportation-project laws that block communities from expanding bike lane infrastructure.
Require DOT to install street safety design measures at dangerous intersections near daycares and schools.
quote: We're putting families at risk unnecessarily.

name: Nyla Rosario
affiliation: One Fair Wage, national policy director and New York state director
proposals: Recommend a charter amendment barring the city from issuing outdoor business licenses to businesses that do not pay a fair living wage.
Place the living-wage license condition before voters on the ballot.
quote: Ensuring that businesses pay a living wage should be viewed as another basic condition for doing business in the city of New York.

name: Geoff Brown
affiliation: Former New York City chief information security officer (2016-2022) and founding head of NYC Cyber Command
proposals: Strengthen the charter to name a commissioner-level official to own cyber risk.
Guarantee that cyber-security official a reporting line independent of IT delivery.
Require public, auditable reporting on the city's cyber security posture.
quote: The official who owns cyber risk must be able to say no.

name: May Frank
affiliation: Transportation Alternatives, Queens organizer
proposals: Reform the major transportation projects process to speed up standard street safety redesigns.
Shorten timelines so DOT can meet the streets plan mandates and Vision Zero goals.
quote: We are not reinventing the wheel, folks. We just need to make it turn faster.

name: Phil Wong
affiliation: New York City Council member (District 30, Queens)
proposals: Support targeted DOB reforms to speed routine commercial and residential permitting.
Require the charter to distinguish routine projects from large high-impact facilities that must get mandatory Phase 2 environmental review before a permit issues.
Retain his 12 June recommendations, including honest multi-year budget baselines and school consolidation for tiny campuses.
quote: Streamlining must not become a shortcut that bypasses environmental and community protections for larger, higher-impact projects.

name: Brendan Griffith
affiliation: New York City Central Labor Council, AFL-CIO, president
proposals: Make partnership with unions a cornerstone by standardizing labor impact reviews and co-developing agency restructuring protocols.
Require agencies to incorporate staffing, retention, training and morale into operational and capital planning.
Strengthen procurement and contract oversight to root out duplicative work and unnecessary outside consulting.
Proceed carefully on any open primaries changes with full transparency.
quote: Efficiency can be a loaded term and it can be a euphemism for austerity.

name: Kevin Lera
affiliation: Make McGuinness Safe, street safety advocate; fourth-generation Greenpoint resident
proposals: Streamline how street safety infrastructure, bus lanes, bike lanes and comprehensive redesigns get built citywide.
quote: I blame a system that prioritizes inertia over action.

name: Selvena Brooks-Powers
affiliation: New York City Council member (southeast Queens and the Rockaway Peninsula)
proposals: Pursue efficiency reforms only where they do not shift authority from the council to the executive.
Keep council notice and review roles for major transportation projects, city office leases and the building code.
Publish clearly defined ballot proposals and complete hearing transcripts before asking voters to decide.
Improve public outreach and advertising for the testimony process.
quote: Efficiency cannot come at the expense of accountability, transparency, or the balance of powers established in our charter.

name: Eric McClure
affiliation: Streets PAC, executive director; chair of Brooklyn Community Board 6
proposals: Support charter revision to reform the major transportation projects process and put street safety on the November ballot.
Remove the 2025 requirement to consult individual firehouses on bike and bus lane projects.
End the two-tiered review that treats short bike and bus lanes as major transportation projects.
quote: Community boards don't vote on whether we upgrade a sewer or water main, and they shouldn't be hamstringing implementation of projects that we know will save lives.

name: Steve Flack
affiliation: Resident, fourth-generation New Yorker and lifelong Brooklyn resident; street safety advocate
proposals: Reform the major transportation projects process to fast-track standard safety redesigns and meet the streets plan benchmarks.
quote: The only thing slower than making these changes is the M42 bus.

name: Xavier Puok
affiliation: Resident; young worker and Brooklyn Technical High School student
proposals: Advance a charter amendment barring the city from issuing or renewing outdoor eating licenses for businesses that refuse to pay a living wage.
quote: Poverty wages don't teach responsibility. They create instability.

name: Laura Shepard
affiliation: Resident
proposals: Eliminate the outdoor dining publishing requirement that forces businesses to buy an ~$800 print newspaper ad.
Put street safety redesigns in the hands of experts and reform the major transportation law.
Streamline the tangled jurisdiction for sidewalk maintenance and speed up Department of Design and Construction violation response.
quote: There is almost no greater example of government inefficiency than the fact that, while the city owns the sidewalks, property owners are responsible for their maintenance and care.

name: Misha Nonnen
affiliation: New York City Criminal Justice Agency, chief program officer (Queens supervised release provider)
proposals: Treat procurement and permitting streamlining as a public safety and justice issue, not only a fiscal one.
Speed human services procurement so community organizations can be paid and open sooner.
Speed permitting so transitional and supportive housing comes online faster.
quote: We also see just as clearly how slow-moving bureaucracy undermines that community strength.

name: Michelle Yanchi
affiliation: Good Shepherd Services, CEO (nonprofit human service provider)
proposals: Amend the charter to fix procurement and contract payment delays that force nonprofits into costly lines of credit.
Require sufficient contract rates and automatic cost escalators tied to the true cost of living.
Hold the city to paying its own contracted human services workers at least a true cost-of-living wage.
quote: We were paid almost $400,000 in interest costs just to maintain our line of credit.

name: Merrill Labour
affiliation: Resident of Greenpoint, Brooklyn; longtime North Brooklyn street safety advocate
proposals: Streamline the street redesign process by cutting paperwork and notification requirements for major transportation projects, including bike and bus lanes over three blocks.
quote: DOT spends more resources on paperwork instead of making our streets the envy of the world.

name: Rex Tai
affiliation: New York City Democratic Socialists of America member (Afro-Socialist and Socialists of Color Caucus); internal medicine physician at NYC Health + Hospitals
proposals: Streamline street safety projects so they move with all due haste.
Consider the Department of Community Safety proposal to move the office into a full department.
quote: Preventive street safety infrastructure keeps neighborhood residents alive, independent, participating in city life rather than being struck by automobiles.

name: Alex Moreno
affiliation: Brooklyn resident; street safety activist and traffic crash survivor
proposals: Adopt charter revisions to streamline street redesign implementation.
Remove the intense multi-agency review and firehouse veto power over bike and bus projects.
quote: It's New Yorkers of color and poor New Yorkers who disproportionately bear these impacts.

name: Pedro Rodriguez
affiliation: Resident; street safety advocate
proposals: Make the city legally liable for damages on streets it knew were dangerous, creating a real enforcement incentive for Vision Zero.
quote: Make Vision Zero bite. It needs to bite back.

name: Karen Wharton
affiliation: Citizen Action of New York, legislative director; convener of the statewide New York BIPOC democracy table
proposals: Oppose putting open primaries on the ballot.
Create a broad-based task force to study the full range of electoral reforms through the lens of participation, racial equity and representation.
quote: Different cities, same promise, same playbook: a sweeping, irreversible change.

name: Ryan Shanahan
affiliation: Vera Institute of Justice, chief research officer
proposals: Add a charter mandate that the Department of Correction is responsible for protecting the human dignity of everyone who works there or is in its care.
quote: Government efficiency isn't just about processes. It's about a clarity of purpose and an alignment of values.

name: Sarah Catalinato
affiliation: Parents to Improve School Transportation (and Student Omni for All)
proposals: Change the governance of pupil transportation to align with the charter's user-focused design mandate.
Put a ballot proposal empowering a permanent commission of parents, educators, bus workers, students and disability advocates to make and implement pupil transportation policy.
quote: OPT spends 2 billion a year making 2-hour routes for 5-year-olds.

name: Alex Kamarda
affiliation: Reinvent Albany, senior policy adviser
proposals: Replace RARBRC with a process where agencies annually propose reports to eliminate via rulemaking plus a public hearing, with the council able to overturn.
Require a four-year sunset for new reporting requirements.
Have the council adopt a protocol to avoid reflexively mandating new reports.
Note support for ranked choice voting and nonpartisan elections.
quote: Just 28 reports have been eliminated since 2010, while over 1,500 new reporting requirements have been mandated.

name: Namaka Gibbi
affiliation: New York City Council, general counsel (testifying on behalf of the speaker)
proposals: Use the full two-year timeline and defer ballot proposals to 2027 rather than rushing.
Engage council subject-matter experts on concrete recommendations before the final report.
Drop proposals that violate the state constitution, such as giving DOB power to amend the building code without council approval or auto-sunsetting council voting authority.
quote: There's nothing efficient about getting the wrong policy outcome.

name: Lee Drutman
affiliation: New America (Washington think tank), political scientist studying electoral reform
proposals: Reject the open primaries proposal, which the academic literature does not support.
Pursue proportional representation for New York City instead to create real competition and representation.
quote: Registering as a Democrat costs nothing. It's as free as a free bus.

name: Briana Strong
affiliation: Resident; independent voter raising her daughter in Brooklyn
proposals: Place an open primaries proposal on the November ballot so voters can decide whether every voter deserves an equal voice.
quote: It is taxation without meaningful representation and it has no place in a city that claims to value democracy.

name: Lourdes Blanco
affiliation: Resident; NYPD school crossing guard testifying on her own behalf (originally from Venezuela)
proposals: Launch stage-one road safety education in schools to build a safety culture from childhood.
Run stage-two community awareness campaigns on respecting traffic signs.
Deploy stage-three smart counting and statistical monitoring devices to identify the most vulnerable intersections.
quote: Road safety education in schools... the goal would be to create a new culture from childhood.

name: Alvin Carter
affiliation: 32BJ SEIU, deputy director of the security division
proposals: Preserve public notification requirements with ample lead time so displaced security workers can invoke displaced-worker ordinances.
Ensure any proposals to limit contract reporting still give workers sufficient notice of contractor changes.
quote: The city and its contracted shelter operators facilitate the lowering of job standards for security officers.

name: Gail Brewer
affiliation: New York City Council member (Upper West Side, Clinton and part of Central Park, Manhattan)
proposals: Leave sidewalk cafe review as-is and wait for Council Member Restler's roadway dining negotiation.
Strengthen DOI and COIB with fixed terms, minimum budgets and no removal of the DOI commissioner.
Move the Marriage Bureau out of the City Clerk's office to COIB.
Keep council-power-usurping and overly complicated items off the ballot and handle them legislatively.
quote: I didn't know what the hell revocable consent is, nor does anybody else.

name: Judy
affiliation: Resident; community public health advocate of over 40 years, testifying as an individual
proposals: Ensure preventive and primary care access for people losing health insurance.
Tap the funds of wealthy voluntary nonprofit hospitals to support safety-net health care providers in underserved communities.
quote: It's penny-wise and pound-foolish to not provide preventive and primary care, particularly to people that are not going to have access.

name: Michelle Jackson
affiliation: Human Services Council, executive director (represents over 180 human service providers)
proposals: Write procurement reform into the charter with clear payment time frames, a requirement that the Procurement Policy Board meet quarterly, and interest penalties.
If this commission cannot take it up, recommend a future charter revision commission focused solely on procurement reform.
Set guideposts constraining OMB's role in approving budgets, invoices and modifications so payment happens on time.
quote: I have been giving the same testimony since 2010 on the charter.

name: Eric Huntley
affiliation: Resident; Black restaurant worker with over 20 years in NYC restaurants (One Fair Wage)
proposals: Put a charter amendment before voters barring outdoor dining licenses for businesses that do not pay their workers a living wage.
quote: I'm not asking for special treatment. I'm asking for dignity.
